summary: Blazing Lost Sailor>Saint>Deal and fierce Shakedown‑Playin’‑Terrapin
blurb: The show delivers a scorching Lost Sailor > Saint > Deal medley and an intense Shakedown Street → Playing In The Band → Terrapin Station run. Jerry Garcia’s leads are jaw‑dropping throughout, while the rhythm section locks in tight grooves. Highlights also include Bob’s rare Spoonful solo and a whistle‑topped Truckin'.
review: On June 30 1984 at the Indianapolis Sports and Music Center the Dead turned in one of the summer’s most memorable performances.  Set 1 built momentum with a seamless Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance → Deal sequence that many fans still cite as a peak‑moment, bolstered by Jerry’s “belting” guitar work and a fiery Ramble On Rose.  The second set exploded into a rare Shakedown Street → Playing In The Band → Terrapin Station marathon; the drum duo rode an unrelenting groove while the band navigated space with uncanny telepathy.  Notable detours include Bob Weir’s unexpected lead on Spoonful and a whistle‑laced Truckin' that lifted the crowd into a dance‑party frenzy.  While a few recordings note minor slip‑ups—most prominently a sloppy feel on the later second‑set in one audience tape—the overwhelming consensus is that this concert captures the Dead at a creative peak, with tight interplay, soaring solos, and the kind of improvisational daring that defines the era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The majority of recordings describe the performance in glowing terms—'jaw‑dropping', 'smoking guitar', 'magical', and praise rare moments like Bob's Spoonful lead. Only one mixed review notes a sloppy second set, so positive sentiment outweighs negatives, warranting a strong +1.2 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with clear, crisp capture of the blistering second set; despite a missing right channel on Shakedown and a cut‑up Day Job, it delivers the most complete and vivid listening experience.

key_highlights: Jaw‑dropping Lost Sailor > Saint Of Circumstance > Deal medley
Fierce Shakedown Street → Playing In The Band → Terrapin Station run
Jerry Garcia’s smoking, nonstop shredding throughout the night
Bob Weir’s rare lead solo on Spoonful and whistle‑topped Truckin'

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers jaw‑dropping leads on Sailor > Saint and a nonstop shredding solo during the Deal jam; his guitar is described as smoking and belting throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Steps out with a rare lead guitar solo on Spoonful and adds a memorable whistle intro to Truckin'.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides a blistering drum solo that drives the second‑set energy, noted for its power despite an odd helicopter searchlight distraction.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
